title: Johnny Reid

infobox.value: * JCD, [DreamWorks](/wiki/DreamWorks_Records "DreamWorks Records"), * [Open Road](/wiki/Open_Road_Recordings "Open Road Recordings"), * [EMI Music Canada](/wiki/EMI "EMI"), * [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan_Records "Manhattan Records")

(born August 21, 1973) is a Scottish-Canadian [country](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") music artist. Reid emigrated to Canada in July 1989 when he was 16 from [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). His father, a diesel mechanic, wanted to give Reid and his brother opportunities he did not think they would have in Scotland. He graduated from [Turner Fenton Secondary School](/wiki/Turner_Fenton_Secondary_School "Turner Fenton Secondary School") in [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario"), in 1992. Reid went on to attend [Bishop's University](/wiki/Bishop%27s_University "Bishop's University") in [Lennoxville](/wiki/Lennoxville "Lennoxville"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), where he met his wife (and was a kicker on the varsity football team).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Scottish-Canadian country music artist

For the Scottish footballer, see [Johnny Reid (footballer)](/wiki/Johnny_Reid_(footballer) "Johnny Reid (footballer)"). For the New Zealand racing driver, see [Jonny Reid](/wiki/Jonny_Reid "Jonny Reid").

| Johnny Reid | |
| --- | --- |
| Johnny Reid in 2015 | |
| Born | (1973-08-21) August 21, 1973 (age 52)  [Lanark](/wiki/Lanark "Lanark"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom")[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Occupation | Singer |
| Years active | 1997–present |
| Labels | * JCD, [DreamWorks](/wiki/DreamWorks_Records "DreamWorks Records"), * [Open Road](/wiki/Open_Road_Recordings "Open Road Recordings"), * [EMI Music Canada](/wiki/EMI "EMI"), * [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan_Records "Manhattan Records") |
| Website | [www.johnnyreid.com](http://www.johnnyreid.com/) |

**John Kirkland Reid** (born August 21, 1973) is a Scottish-Canadian [country](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") music artist. Reid emigrated to Canada in July 1989 when he was 16 from [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"), [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). His father, a diesel mechanic, wanted to give Reid and his brother opportunities he did not think they would have in Scotland. He graduated from [Turner Fenton Secondary School](/wiki/Turner_Fenton_Secondary_School "Turner Fenton Secondary School") in [Brampton](/wiki/Brampton "Brampton"), [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario"), in 1992. Reid went on to attend [Bishop's University](/wiki/Bishop%27s_University "Bishop's University") in [Lennoxville](/wiki/Lennoxville "Lennoxville"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), where he met his wife (and was a kicker on the varsity football team).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Reid moved to [Nashville, Tennessee](/wiki/Nashville,_Tennessee "Nashville, Tennessee") to make country music; he and his family reside in Nashville.[[3]](#cite_note-The_Sunday_Post-3) He was nominated for the 2008 [Juno Award](/wiki/Juno_Award "Juno Award") for Country Recording of the Year for *Kicking Stones*. Reid was named Male Artist of the Year at both the 2008 and 2009 [CCMA](/wiki/Canadian_Country_Music_Association "Canadian Country Music Association") Awards. He also performed at the Canada Day celebrations on Parliament Hill on July 1, 2010, which the [Queen](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II") and [Prince Philip](/wiki/Prince_Philip "Prince Philip") attended.

## Music career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Music career")]

### *Another Day, Another Dime* and *Johnny Reid*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Another Day, Another Dime and Johnny Reid")]

Reid's debut album, *Another Day, Another Dime*, was released in 1997 on JCD Records. No singles were ever released from the album. However, it was his second album, which was self-titled and released in 2000, where he saw minimal chart success. Three singles from this album were released and all three of which charted. The lead-off and debut single, "Given Up on Me", was a minor hit on the *[RPM](/wiki/RPM_(magazine) "RPM (magazine)")* Country Tracks chart. It reached a peak of No. 45. A second single, "Runnin' Wild" charted at No. 50. The album's third and final single, "She Don't Wanna Hear", just missed the Top 40 of the country chart, peaking at No. 41.

### [*Born to Roll*](/wiki/Born_to_Roll_(album) "Born to Roll (album)")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Born to Roll")]

Reid's first single in four years, "[You Still Own Me](/wiki/You_Still_Own_Me "You Still Own Me")", was released in 2004. It was the lead-off single to his third studio album, *[Born to Roll](/wiki/Born_to_Roll_(album) "Born to Roll (album)")*. The charted in the 20 of the Canadian country charts. *Born to Roll* was released on March 15, 2005, via [Open Road Recordings](/wiki/Open_Road_Recordings "Open Road Recordings"), and peaked at No. 5 on the Canadian Top Country Albums chart. A second single, "Sixty to Zero", charted to No. 19, however it was the album's third single to get Reid into the Top 10 of the charts. "Missing an Angel" eventually reached No. 6 on the charts, giving him his first Top 10 single of his career. A fourth single, "Time Flies", also managed to chart in the Top 10, peaking at No. 9. A fifth and final single, "Gypsy in My Soul", only made No. 12. *Born to Roll* became his first certified album, being certified Gold by the [Canadian Recording Industry Association](/wiki/Canadian_Recording_Industry_Association "Canadian Recording Industry Association") (CRIA).

In 2007, Canadian music group [Emerson Drive](/wiki/Emerson_Drive "Emerson Drive") covered "You Still Own Me". They took the song to No. 22 on the U.S. *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* [Hot Country Songs](/wiki/Hot_Country_Songs "Hot Country Songs") chart.

### *[Kicking Stones](/wiki/Kicking_Stones "Kicking Stones")* and [*Dance with Me*](/wiki/Dance_with_Me_(Johnny_Reid_album) "Dance with Me (Johnny Reid album)")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Kicking Stones and Dance with Me")]

In 2007, a new single entitled "Love Sweet Love", would be released and act as the lead-off single to *[Kicking Stones](/wiki/Kicking_Stones "Kicking Stones")*, which was released on April 10, 2007. The single peaked at No. 11 on the Canadian country charts and was followed by a string of Top 20 singles. The second single, the album's title track, reached No. 13, and "[Darlin'](/wiki/Darlin%27_(Poacher_song) "Darlin' (Poacher song)")", the album's third single, became Reid's first Top 5 single. Two additional singles, "Thank You" and "Out of the Blue", were released, peaking at No. 13 and No. 7 on the charts, respectively.

Reid's fifth studio album, *[Dance with Me](/wiki/Dance_with_Me_(Johnny_Reid_album) "Dance with Me (Johnny Reid album)")*, was released in March 2009, and debuted at No. 1 on the Canadian Top Country Albums chart and No. 3 on the [Canadian Albums Chart](/wiki/Canadian_Albums_Chart "Canadian Albums Chart").[[4]](#cite_note-4) "[A Woman Like You](/wiki/A_Woman_Like_You_(Johnny_Reid_song) "A Woman Like You (Johnny Reid song)")" was released as the first single from the album and reached No. 4 on the country charts, peaking No. 4 in 2009. The title track was then released and reached No. 10, followed by "Old Flame", which has so far peaked in the Top 10.

A Christmas album, entitled *[Christmas](/wiki/Christmas_(Johnny_Reid_album) "Christmas (Johnny Reid album)")*, was released November 10, 2009. Reid's first live DVD, *Live at the Jubilee* was released on February 9, 2010.

### [*A Place Called Love*](/wiki/A_Place_Called_Love_(Johnny_Reid_album) "A Place Called Love (Johnny Reid album)")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: A Place Called Love")]

In 2010, Johnny Reid signed with [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI") for the release of his next studio album, *[A Place Called Love](/wiki/A_Place_Called_Love_(Johnny_Reid_album) "A Place Called Love (Johnny Reid album)")*, released August 31, 2010. The first single, "[Today I'm Gonna Try and Change the World](/wiki/Today_I%27m_Gonna_Try_and_Change_the_World "Today I'm Gonna Try and Change the World")" was released on June 7. Reid's second live DVD, *Heart & Soul* was released on March 29, 2011, in Canada.

### [*Fire It Up*](/wiki/Fire_It_Up_(Johnny_Reid_album) "Fire It Up (Johnny Reid album)")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Fire It Up")]

2012 marked the release of Johnny Reid's second album for EMI, *[Fire It Up](/wiki/Fire_It_Up_(Johnny_Reid_album) "Fire It Up (Johnny Reid album)")*. The [title track](/wiki/Fire_It_Up_(Johnny_Reid_song) "Fire It Up (Johnny Reid song)") was released on January 10 to Canadian radio and [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes"). The album features Canadian guest artists [Carolyn Dawn Johnson](/wiki/Carolyn_Dawn_Johnson "Carolyn Dawn Johnson") and [Serena Ryder](/wiki/Serena_Ryder "Serena Ryder"). The album was also released as a deluxe version with four bonus songs and a DVD featuring the making of the album.

Reid released his second Christmas album, *[A Christmas Gift to You](/wiki/A_Christmas_Gift_to_You "A Christmas Gift to You")*, on October 22, 2013.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### *[What Love Is All About](/wiki/What_Love_Is_All_About "What Love Is All About")*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: What Love Is All About")]

Reid's first studio album for [Universal Music Canada](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group"), *[What Love Is All About](/wiki/What_Love_Is_All_About "What Love Is All About")*, was released on November 13, 2015. The album features a duet with [Kardinal Offishall](/wiki/Kardinal_Offishall "Kardinal Offishall"). Reid's first single off the album was "A Picture of You". It was followed by "Honey Honey".

### *Revival*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Revival")]

Reid released *Revival* with [Universal Music Canada](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") on November 3, 2017. The album featured 13 original songs and marked Reid's most soul-inspired album to date. In 2018, Revival was nominated for two JUNO Awards in the categories of Album of the Year and Adult Contemporary Album of the Year. On November 9, 2018, Reid released *Revival Live*, his first live album, which was recorded during the 2018 Revival Live Canadian tour.

## Musical theatre

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Musical theatre")]

Main article: [Maggie (Reid musical)](/wiki/Maggie_(Reid_musical) "Maggie (Reid musical)")

In 2016, Reid first began working on a musical that was inspired by the real-life story of his grandmother, Maggie.[[6]](#cite_note-Gallant2022-6) Originally titled *My Bonnie Lass*, the show was developed by Canadian producer [Michael Rubinoff](/wiki/Michael_Rubinoff "Michael Rubinoff") at [Sheridan College](/wiki/Sheridan_College "Sheridan College")’s prestigious  [Canadian Music Theatre Project](/wiki/Michael_Rubinoff#Canadian_Music_Theatre_Project "Michael Rubinoff") in 2018, followed by a staged workshop in 2019.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The musical, retitled  *[Maggie](/wiki/Maggie_(Reid_musical) "Maggie (Reid musical)")*, premiered at Theatre Aquarius in [Hamilton, Ontario](/wiki/Hamilton,_Ontario "Hamilton, Ontario") in 2023, and then also played at the [Charlottetown Festival](/wiki/Charlottetown_Festival "Charlottetown Festival") and the [Savoy Theatre](/wiki/Savoy_Theatre,_Glace_Bay "Savoy Theatre, Glace Bay").[[6]](#cite_note-Gallant2022-6) Later that year, a studio cast album was released that featured the cast of the Sheridan College workshop. In 2024, *Maggie* premiered in the United States where it played at the  [Goodspeed Opera House](/wiki/Goodspeed_Musicals "Goodspeed Musicals") between August 23 and October 20, 2024.[[8]](#cite_note-Culwell-Block-8)

## Band

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johnny_Reid&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Band")]

* Ben Rutz (fiddle)
* Michael Shand (keyboards)
* Yvan Petit (lead guitar)
* Mark Selby (guitar)
* Charles Fields (drums/percussion)
* Nick Czarnogorski (bass) Regular session musician for [Fatlabs](/wiki/Fatlabs "Fatlabs") studios. Also plays in a band named Ride The Tiger.
* Saidah Baba Talibah (backup vocals)
* Miku Graham (backup vocals)
* Quisha Wint (backup vocals)
* Jon Jackson (sax)
* Chris West (sax)
* Jon-Paul Frappier (trumpet)
* Oscar Utterstrom (trombone)
